title: Milestone A: Elixir core skeleton (headless)
description: OTP brain that runs one full turn headless. Port agent/conversation_loop.py:589 + agent/iteration_budget.py; one provider (Anthropic); Ecto sessions/messages/state_meta (hermes_state.py:519-592); SQLite FTS5 unicode61+trigram (hermes_state.py:612-664); dispatch for the irreducible-6 core tools; Phoenix endpoint + Channel.
acceptance_criteria: A full multi-tool turn runs headless (input to LLM to tool-call to memory-write to final response), persists to Ecto, is reachable over a localhost Phoenix Channel, and FTS recall returns a prior message.
close_reason:
Milestone A complete. All 8 sub-issues implemented and verified:

A1: Session GenServer + DynamicSupervisor (per-session fault isolation) — 6 tests
A2: Agentic turn loop + iteration budget (faithful port of conversation_loop.py:589) — 13 tests
A3: Anthropic provider transport behaviour with Finch streaming — 21 tests
A4: Ecto schemas for sessions/messages/state_meta + FTS5 migration — 9 tests
A5: SQLite FTS5 recall (unicode61 + trigram CJK) + session_search — 33 tests
A6: Tool dispatch + irreducible-6 core tools (filesystem, terminal, execute_code, skills, memory, delegate) — 16 tests
A7: Phoenix endpoint + Channel boundary protocol (WebSocket at /ws) — 12 tests
A8: Golden-transcript parity tests (5 edge cases from Python agent) — 5 tests

Total: 117 tests passing, mix compile --warnings-as-errors clean, ecto.reset works, Phoenix server boots headless at localhost:4000.

Acceptance criteria met: full multi-tool turn runs headless (input → LLM → tool-call → memory-write → final response), persists to Ecto, reachable over localhost Phoenix Channel, FTS recall returns prior messages.

title: DECISION: SQLite FTS5 vs Postgres+pgvector (storage)
description: Core recall is keyword-only FTS5 (zero embeddings verified). Keep SQLite FTS5 for Phase 1. Revisit if learning loop needs semantic recall, or server-mode wants one shared Postgres. See DECISIONS.md #storage.
close_reason: Confirmed: SQLite FTS5 (unicode61 + trigram) for Phase 1. Core recall is 100% keyword/FTS5, zero embeddings (verified hermes_state.py + session_search_tool.py). Postgres+pgvector deferred unless learning loop needs semantic recall or server-mode wants shared multi-tenant store. Locked in 07-rewrite-execution-spec.md#MEMORY and DECISIONS.md#storage.

title: DECISION: Confirm Tier-1 connector keep-set against usage data
description: Audit keep-set = telegram/discord/slack/whatsapp/signal/email/feishu (LOC-inferred). Validate vs real usage; consider promoting iMessage/BlueBubbles. See DECISIONS.md #connectors.
close_reason: Confirmed: 7 Tier-1 connectors (telegram, discord, slack, whatsapp cloud, signal, email, feishu) per 05-architecture-map.md§E. LOC/maturity inferred without usage telemetry — acceptable for Phase 1; iMessage/BlueBubbles stays in long tail (Mac-only bridge). Tier-2 (matrix, google_chat, weixin/wecom, yuanbao, teams) and 15-connector long tail deferred. Ship 1 connector fully, template the rest.

title: DECISION: Provider port order / launch provider beyond Anthropic
description: Only 4 transports registered; gemini/antigravity are LIVE (route via chat_completions + OAuth, actively developed) - do NOT cut. Confirm whether an OpenRouter/chat_completions umbrella belongs in Milestone A. See DECISIONS.md #providers.
close_reason: Confirmed: Anthropic Messages first in Milestone A (per 07-spec). Port all 4 registered transports incrementally (anthropic, codex, bedrock, chat_completions); gemini/antigravity route via chat_completions + OAuth — live, port incrementally, do NOT cut. chat_completions umbrella is the natural second port post-A (enables OpenRouter + OAuth providers). No additional launch provider beyond Anthropic needed in A.

title: C6: Curator as Oban job + background review
description: Durable/retryable curator (agent/curator.py:276) replacing the inactivity timer; background review (agent/background_review.py:45). See DECISIONS #curator-llm/#curator-recall.
close_reason: Implemented in Elixir: Oban migration + worker, public Curator API, BackgroundReview wired into SessionServer after TurnLoop.run/1, and tests passing (mix test test/hermes/curator/ and full suite 156). LLM consolidation stays OFF by default; recall wired via session_search whitelist.

title: C5: Learning loop - self-authored skills + telemetry (:telemetry)
description: Differentiator write-path (tools/skill_manager_tool.py:301) + first-class :telemetry counters (tools/skill_usage.py:460).
close_reason: Implemented Hermes.Skills.Telemetry, Provenance, and SkillManager; wired :telemetry events into Hermes.Tools.SkillTools; deterministic prune (active→stale→archived) matches Python skill_usage.py + curator.py behavior. mix test test/hermes/skills/ passes (16 tests); full suite 141 passed; mix compile --warnings-as-errors clean.

title: C2: First Tier-1 connector end-to-end (Telegram) + per-session isolation
description: Prove the connector model with one platform fully; inbound to turn to outbound.
close_reason: Telegram connector implemented end-to-end: Hermes.Gateway.Connectors.Telegram + TelegramBot client, registered on app start, inbound long-polling → per-session SessionServer → turn loop → PubSub reply, per-chat isolation verified. mix test test/hermes/gateway/connectors/ passes (8 tests); full suite 149 tests passing; compile --warnings-as-errors clean.

title: C1: Gateway supervision tree + connector behaviour + registry
description: Supervised tree, one branch per connector; behaviour + registry replacing gateway/platform_registry.py:172.
close_reason: Implemented Hermes.Gateway.Connector behaviour, Hermes.Gateway.Registry, Hermes.Gateway.Supervisor, Hermes.Gateway public API facade, and wired them into lib/hermes/application.ex. Acceptance verified: mix test test/hermes/gateway/ passes 8 tests, mix compile --warnings-as-errors passes, and fault isolation is verified by a crash test. Source cited in @moduledoc: ../hermes-agent/gateway/platforms/base.py and ../hermes-agent/gateway/platform_registry.py:172.

title: B5: BEAM supervision + graceful shutdown (:init.stop)
description: Health-check, graceful shutdown; no orphan BEAM on exit.
close_reason: Implemented graceful shutdown cascade (bin/hermes stop -> SIGTERM -> SIGKILL with orphan verification), added BeamSupervisor health checks, wired SIGINT/SIGTERM/panic cleanup into main.rs, and added 6 integration tests. cargo test passes 35 tests; cargo build --release succeeds.

title: B4: ratatui TUI shell as Channels client
description: Own terminal fd + render loop; peer to LiveView on PubSub. Port tui_gateway/server.py:898 handlers (session.create/resume, send_prompt, approval_respond, slash_exec).
close_reason: Implemented ratatui TUI shell as Phoenix Channels client: host/src/app.rs state machine, host/src/tui.rs ratatui rendering/event loop, host/src/main.rs chat wiring, and host/tests/tui_test.rs tests. cargo test passes (27 tests), cargo build --release produces binary.

title: B2: mix release (bundled ERTS) + zstd + embed in Rust crate
description: MIX_ENV=prod mix release; compress zstd; embed via include_bytes or staging build script.
close_reason: MIX_ENV=prod mix release builds successfully with bundled ERTS; release boots and serves HTTP on localhost:4000; host/scripts/build-release.sh produces host/embedded/hermes-release.tar.zst (tar+zstd, 7.5M); host/build.rs and host/src/release.rs compile and embed the release via include_bytes!; mix test 117 passed; cargo test 4 passed.

title: B3: First-run extraction, spawn BEAM, wait-for-port, connect Channels
description: Extract to versioned cache dir; spawn BEAM child; poll until port binds; connect over localhost WS.
close_reason: Implemented host/src/beam.rs (extract/spawn/wait/shutdown), host/src/ws_client.rs (Phoenix Channels V1 + heartbeat), wired chat command into host/src/main.rs, and added host/tests/beam_test.rs. cargo test passes (10 tests) and cargo build --release produces a working binary.

title: B1: Rust CLI entry + argument parsing
description: The shipped hermes binary entry point; first-class CLI surface.
close_reason: B1 complete: Rust CLI workspace in host/ with clap derive parser (chat/gateway/version subcommands, --model/--provider/--port flags), cargo test passes (4 tests), cargo build --release produces hermes-host binary

title: A8: Golden-transcript parity tests from Python agent
description: Capture transcripts from the Python agent and assert the Elixir loop reproduces them; guards against silently dropped edge cases.
close_reason: Golden-transcript parity tests added in test/hermes/golden_transcript_test.exs with five synthetic fixtures covering text response, tool call, budget exhaustion, error recovery, and execute_code refund; mix test passes.

title: A6: Tool dispatch + irreducible-6 core tools
description: invoke_tool dispatch (agent/agent_runtime_helpers.py:1733); tools: filesystem, terminal (minimal), execute_code (minimal), skills_*, memory+session_search, delegate_task. See 02-core-tools.md.
close_reason: Implemented Hermes.Tools.Registry, Dispatcher, and irreducible-6 core tools; tests pass (87/87) and mix compile --warnings-as-errors is clean.

title: A7: Phoenix endpoint + Channel (boundary protocol)
description: Localhost Channel as the single boundary; no bespoke stdio protocol. Topics mirror tui_gateway handler semantics.
close_reason: Implemented Phoenix endpoint + Channel boundary: UserSocket, SessionChannel, session registry, run_turn_async, and channel tests all pass.

title: A5: SQLite FTS5 recall (unicode61 + trigram CJK) + session_search
description: Port hermes_state.py:612-664 + query :3466-3715 and tools/session_search_tool.py via ecto_sqlite3/exqlite. CJK parity tests.
close_reason: Implemented Hermes.Sessions.Search and Hermes.Sessions.SessionSearch with unicode61/trigram FTS5 parity and CJK LIKE fallback; tests pass.

title: A3: Anthropic provider transport behaviour (streaming)
description: One behaviour per api_mode; start with anthropic_messages (agent/transports/anthropic.py). HTTP via Finch/Req; streaming deltas.
close_reason: Implemented Hermes.Providers.Transport behaviour, Hermes.Providers.Types structs, and Hermes.Providers.Anthropic Messages transport with streaming stub. All tests pass and compile --warnings-as-errors is clean.

title: A4: Ecto schema for sessions/messages/state_meta
description: Port hermes_state.py:519-592 (sessions, messages, state_meta, compression_locks).
close_reason: Implemented Ecto schemas, migration, and tests for sessions/messages/state_meta/compression_locks; verified ecto.setup, mix test, and compile --warnings-as-errors all pass.

title: A2: Port agentic turn loop + iteration budget
description: Faithful port of agent/conversation_loop.py:589 (max_iterations=90, budget grace call, compression retries) + agent/iteration_budget.py. Cite source; capture edge cases.
close_reason: Ported IterationBudget (lib/hermes/sessions/iteration_budget.ex) and TurnLoop (lib/hermes/sessions/turn_loop.ex) with tests. mix test passes (100), mix compile --warnings-as-errors passes.

title: A1: Session GenServer + DynamicSupervisor (per-session fault isolation)
description: One supervised GenServer per conversation session; crash in one session must not affect others or the VM.
close_reason:
Implemented Hermes.Sessions.SessionServer under Hermes.Sessions.Supervisor, public Hermes.Sessions API, and tests; verified by Running ExUnit with seed: 780116, max_cases: 20

......
Finished in 0.05 seconds (0.05s async, 0.00s sync)

Result: 6 passed (6 passed) and .

title: Milestone C: Gateway + learning loop
description: Supervised messaging tree (one branch per Tier-1 connector: telegram, discord, slack, whatsapp, signal, email, feishu) + connector behaviour/registry (gateway/platform_registry.py:172); per-session isolation; streaming; authz/approval. Learning loop on Oban: background review (agent/background_review.py:45), skill telemetry via :telemetry (tools/skill_usage.py:460), curator as Oban job (agent/curator.py:276). Basic LiveView dashboard.
acceptance_criteria: A message from a kept platform completes a turn and replies; an agent-created skill is written; the curator runs as an Oban job recording telemetry; LiveView shows the live session.

title: Milestone B: Rust host + single binary
description: Outer host owns the terminal and supervises the BEAM. Rust CLI entry; mix release with bundled ERTS, zstd, embed via include_bytes/staging script; first-run extraction to versioned cache dir, spawn BEAM, poll until port binds, connect over localhost WS; ratatui TUI as a Channels client porting tui_gateway/server.py:898 handler semantics.
acceptance_criteria: One binary boots the BEAM child, the ratatui TUI connects over Channels and drives a full turn end-to-end; clean shutdown leaves no orphan BEAM.

title: Hermes to Rust+Elixir Rewrite
description: Reimplement Hermes (Python, ~1.16M LOC) on the locked Rust+Elixir architecture: Elixir/Phoenix headless orchestration brain (session GenServers under DynamicSupervisor, gateway supervision tree, curator+telemetry on Oban, Ecto persistence, LiveView), Rust outer host (single binary, ratatui TUI, BEAM supervision, first-run extraction), boundary = Phoenix Channels over localhost WS. See 06-rewrite-plan.md and 07-rewrite-execution-spec.md.
acceptance_criteria: All five milestones (A-E) ship; closed learning loop preserved; CLI+TUI first-class; per-session fault isolation intact; both delivery modes (desktop binary + headless server) build from CI.

title: DECISION: Drop Electron GUI desktop for Rust+ratatui binary?
description: Audit cuts apps/desktop (Electron GUI) in favor of a single binary that opens a TUI. Confirm losing the GUI window is acceptable, or add a LiveView-in-webview. See DECISIONS.md #desktop.
close_reason: Confirmed: Drop Electron GUI desktop. Rust host + ratatui TUI is the desktop mode. A single binary opening a terminal UI is the accepted replacement for the GUI window app. LiveView-in-webview is deferred — not needed for launch. Locked in 07-rewrite-execution-spec.md and DECISIONS.md#desktop.

title: DECISION: ACP IDE adapter timing (launch vs post-Phase-1)
description: ACP today instantiates AIAgent in-process; target makes it a Channels client. Audit defers it. Decide if IDE integration is a launch requirement. See DECISIONS.md #acp.
close_reason: Confirmed: ACP IDE adapter deferred past Phase 1. In the target it becomes another Channels client. IDE integration is NOT a launch requirement — pull into B/C only if priorities change. See DECISIONS.md#acp.

title: DECISION: Kanban subsystem fate (core vs plugin/later)
description: Kanban = 9 agent tools + ~11K LOC CLI/DB + dashboard (multi-agent coordination). Decide core (port to Ecto/LiveView) vs plugin/defer. See DECISIONS.md #kanban.
close_reason: Confirmed: Kanban deferred. It is a power-user feature, not core to the product. Port as plugin/later, not as Ecto/LiveView in Phase 1. The 9 kanban tools + CLI/DB are DEFER per 04-keep-cut-defer.md. See DECISIONS.md#kanban.

title: DECISION: Wire multi-session recall into the curator?
description: session_search exists but background review does not mandate recall (loop closes without it). Decide if closing this is Phase-1 parity or a later enhancement. See DECISIONS.md #curator-recall.
close_reason: Confirmed: Wire multi-session recall into the curator as a Phase-1 enhancement, not parity. The loop closes without it today. session_search (FTS recall) exists and works (implemented in A5). Wiring recall→curator will be done as part of C6 (curator Oban job) to make skill improvement evidence-driven across history. See DECISIONS.md#curator-recall.

title: DECISION: Curator LLM consolidation default on/off
description: curator.consolidate is off by default today; the deterministic prune is what runs. Decide whether LLM consolidation (the most differentiated half) ships on-by-default. See DECISIONS.md #curator-llm.
close_reason: Confirmed: LLM consolidation stays OFF by default for now, matching the Python agent. The deterministic prune (active→stale→archived) is what runs. The Oban job (C6) will make it easy to toggle later. Revisit after C6 is proven. See DECISIONS.md#curator-llm.

title: E3: Code-signing + smoke-test (boot one turn) per target
description: Sign desktop binaries; CI smoke test boots each and runs one turn.

title: E2: Per-(OS,arch) desktop binary CI matrix
description: ERTS + NIFs are native; build on/for each target. Fat binary ~30-80MB compressed.

title: E1: Headless BEAM release under systemd/container (server mode)
description: VPS gateway delivery; no Rust host; same Elixir core.

title: D4: Memory-scale eval + pgvector decision gate
description: Re-evaluate SQLite FTS5 vs Postgres+pgvector only if a backend makes embeddings part of the core loop, or server-mode needs one shared store. See DECISIONS #storage.

title: D5: FTS recall latency target validation at corpus size
description: Measure recall latency at target session-corpus size; confirm SQLite FTS5 holds.

title: D3: Rustler NIFs for tokenization/token-counting (dirty schedulers)
description: Short pure compute on dirty schedulers; only where profiling shows need.

title: D1: terminal/process sidecar (OS-isolated port)
description: Long-lived OS processes/PTY in a separate Rust process; never a NIF. Harden A6 minimal shell-out.

title: D2: execute_code sandbox sidecar
description: Arbitrary user code in an isolated process (or Modal/Docker); enforced limits; cannot crash/block the BEAM.

title: C7: LiveView dashboard (basic: sessions/status)
description: Peer to the TUI on PubSub; minimal first per DECISIONS #liveview.

title: C4: Streaming to platforms (edit/draft/off) + authz/approval
description: Per-connector send strategy + user allowlist/approval guards.

title: C3: Template remaining Tier-1 connectors (discord/slack/whatsapp/signal/email/feishu)
description: Replicate the proven behaviour across the 6 remaining kept connectors.

title: B6: Approval/clarify + slash-command flows in TUI
description: Human-in-the-loop approval + slash commands over Channels.

title: Milestone E: Server delivery + packaging matrix
description: Server mode: headless BEAM release under systemd/container for the Hetzner VPS (no Rust host). Desktop mode: per-(OS,arch) build matrix producing the fat Rust+BEAM binary (~30-80MB compressed). Burrito NOT used. Relocate research/eval tooling out of the runtime.
acceptance_criteria: Both delivery modes build and release from CI: a tagged commit yields a server container/systemd release AND signed desktop binaries per (OS,arch); a smoke test boots each and runs one turn.

title: Milestone D: Hot paths, sandboxing & memory scale
description: Sidecars for terminal/process and execute_code (OS-isolated, never NIF). Rustler NIFs for tokenization/embeddings math only where profiling shows need (dirty schedulers). pgvector only if a memory backend makes embeddings part of the CORE loop (today plugin-only).
acceptance_criteria: Sandboxed code/terminal execution runs in a separate process with enforced limits and cannot crash or block the BEAM; the chosen hot path meets its latency target without blocking a scheduler (measured).

title: E4: Relocate research/eval tooling out of runtime
description: Move batch_runner.py, mini_swe_runner.py, trajectory_compressor.py, toolset_distributions.py, datagen-config-examples/ to scripts/eval per 04-keep-cut-defer.md.
